notes: Conservative starter relationship set. All relationships must explain what matches, what differs, warnings, confidence and evidence status.

warnings: AG13 is commonly cross-referenced with LR44 but chemistry and voltage must be confirmed.
AG13 (alkaline 1.5V) and LR44 (alkaline 1.5V) are physically similar, but some LR44-cross-referenced products are silver oxide (SR44/357 at 1.55V).
Device compatibility depends on chemistry and discharge profile tolerance.
Always confirm with device manufacturer documentation.
unknowns: Whether device is tolerant of silver-oxide vs alkaline discharge profile
Manufacturer approval for the specific device

warnings: CR2032 (3.2mm) is thicker than CR2025 (2.5mm). Physical fit in the device compartment must be confirmed before use.
CR2032 typically has higher capacity than CR2025 due to greater volume.
Contact spring pressure may be affected by the height difference.
Do not force a CR2032 into a CR2025 compartment.
unknowns: Manufacturer approval or guidance for this substitution

evidence.claim: Both batteries share 20mm diameter and 3V nominal voltage; height differs (2.5mm vs 3.2mm)

warnings: DIN66 and LN2 are sometimes cross-referenced in Australian retail but are not universally interchangeable.
Terminal polarity, hold-down style and connector position can differ between DIN and LN variants.
Never assume interchangeability without explicit OEM or supplier confirmation.
Chemistry requirements (flooded vs AGM vs EFB) must be verified separately.
unknowns: Whether specific DIN66 and LN2 variants share terminal layout
Hold-down ledge compatibility for the specific vehicle

warnings: LR41 (alkaline 1.5V) and SR41 (silver oxide 1.55V) are physically the same size.
The 0.05V nominal voltage difference and significantly different discharge profiles mean they are NOT equivalent in all devices.
Precision instruments and medical devices may require the specific chemistry.
unknowns: Whether device specifications require alkaline or silver oxide

warnings: LR44 (alkaline 1.5V) and SR44 (silver oxide 1.55V) share the same physical dimensions.
Discharge profile differs significantly: SR44 maintains flat voltage, LR44 gradually declines.
Use in precision devices (watches, medical) typically requires silver oxide (SR44/357).
Alkaline substitute may reduce device performance or accuracy.
unknowns: Whether device is sensitive to voltage stability

warnings: AA NiMH (1.2V nominal) has a lower nominal voltage than AA alkaline (1.5V).
NiMH requires a compatible NiMH charger and must not be used with alkaline-only chargers.
Some devices may not operate correctly at 1.2V nominal voltage.
NiMH is rechargeable; alkaline is typically single-use.
